title: What Americans Need to Understand About China
summary: Ezra Klein discusses Xi Jinping’s China with former Australian Prime Minister Kevin Rudd. The two cover how Xi has doubled down on communist ideology and significantly consolidated his own power while transforming China into an industrial juggernaut unrivaled in the world.

title: China Is Sabotaging the World That Enables Its Rise
summary: Following state visits by Trump and Putin to Beijing in May, Chinese state media emphasized the symbolism of foreign leaders paying tribute to Xi Jinping rather than concrete outcomes. The authors argue this reflects Beijing's narrative that China has surpassed its rivals and reclaimed centrality on the world stage.

title: China exports its problems
summary: China's economy grew just 4.3 percent in the second quarter, missing official targets, as traditional sectors like construction shrink rather than merely slow. Fierce competition in emerging industries such as batteries and robotics are squeezing profits, a phenomenon known domestically as "neijuan." An economist at Tsinghua University estimates real unemployment near 10 percent, far above the official 5 percent...

title: This Satellite Imagery reveals just how advanced China's military is
summary: Satellite imagery analyzed by the Telegraph reveals China constructing detailed replicas of US Navy vessels, including an Arleigh Burke class destroyer, in the remote Taklamakan desert. Though 2,700 kilometers from the sea, the models feature masts and radar equipment closely matching the originals. Analysts say the structures let Beijing rehearse missile strikes with precision, perhaps in preparation to...

title: China’s Transnational Repression Is Being Met with a Deafening Silence
summary: The PRC detained another American citizen, Min Zin, on espionage charges, in yet another example of the CCP’s expanding transnational repression campaign. The article argues the Trump administration has stayed largely silent, prioritizing trade relations with Beijing over defending detained or targeted Americans, and calls for sanctions and clearer travel advisories for Americans travelling to the PRC.

title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
summary: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

title: Divide grows between AI employees and executives over policy battles

title: Hegseth’s testosterone mandate carries risks, raises questions

title: Military Service Records, Awards, and Unit Histories: A Guide to Locating Sources

title: Pooling Europe's compute
summary: Frontier AI training has needed compute at one site, hard in Europe given power, chip and political limits. RAND Europe finds distributed training eases the power and political constraints and pools Europe's scattered chips, but adds no new chips.
